compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review triage (run 1: PASS, 0 blocking, 19 advisory). All advisories remediated by amending the description EXCEPT one, justified here: the DET ac-quality finding that S-item criteria bundle deliverables with 'and' — deliberate house epic style (identical to 1fa8/smoky-flare-slug): each S-item becomes a child story at decomposition time whose own AC split into independently verifiable items. Amendments applied: commit-msg hook install prerequisite in merge-back recipe + S4 troubleshooting (ff11e095/f5c13d22); S6 cost/latency made observable — ADR 'Cost & latency' section with named metrics from S5 runs (fb0fb1e0); S2 explicit edit targets incl. voter.py branch-before-get_patch (f20d1038); files/-default-base fixture verification promoted to S2's first step, the plan's riskiest assumption (fa7c0e45); merge detection located inside review_and_vote transparent to webhook/reconciler//rerun + reconcile.py no-change asserted by test (f5ee6ca9/f465b50d); g2p branch-agnostic contradiction resolved — expectation in design, verified statement + run-log done-definition in S3 (fdbcd23d/f86c0cb5/fabc6d3d/ff2275d1); merge-context schema assemble_merge_change_context co-located in assemble.py reusing DIFF_CHAR_CAP, empty-diff case defined (f0b92e4d/f2ee1060); size-cap apportionment specified — combined cap, mergelist capped first at 100 subjects (f7d322ee); robot-comment template via _TAG_SUFFIXES merge-change variant (f0ee6494); observability — structured log events + observability.sh metric + runbook signals (fd731572/fee8b0e7); Push Merge Commit bound to named feature-branch-drivers group (f299aa08); submit-type pin transition safety — record prior value, quiet window, distinct back-out (fc6647bc); vague 'clean' success criterion replaced with recorded hit-list check (f607dd45 partial).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review triage (run 2: PASS, 0 blocking, 20 advisory). FIXED by amendment: data-flow into gate machinery pinned — assembled context passes as diff_text through the existing adapter seam, no runner changes (fcb41bff/f1dbb1fd); metric/log names specified — journald events + review_bot_merge_change_errors via observability.sh (f9865424); _TAG_SUFFIXES semantics — reuse existing verdict keys, suffix appended, no new vocabulary (fe71742e/f0f5a950); setup-project.sh want-dict extension named as S1 target for feature-branch-drivers (fcc4cb2e/fe8e7624); extra commit GET on hot path accepted + documented, fails closed (f4a69748); mergelist/any-merge-REST-failure fallback pinned — fail-closed -1 with coverage-gap tag (fb1a8198/fcaeb72c); abandoned/stale-branch inventory policy added to S6 (f4ed4381); merge-conflict + abandon-merge-change guidance added to S4 recipe (fe5968cd); MERGELIST_MAX_COMMITS named constant (f3f52706); bot-code rollback in S6 + runbook (f3523579); per-file diff fan-out bounded by the combined cap (f0122315). JUSTIFIED, not fixed: g2p ini investigation cannot be resolved pre-1fa8 — g2p is not installed; S3 carries the verified-statement done-definition (f0b0d8da); webhook branch-agnosticism is config-confirmed AND live-proven by S5 scenario 1's done-definition (f55167b8); gerrit-verify.yaml intentionally does not exist yet — an explicit 1fa8 dependency assumption stated in Context (fc2ae55d); MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE acceptance now pinned to S1 (config push) + S5 scenario 4 (live carry) (f7e29888 — partially fixed); DET compound-criteria style — house epic convention, S-items decompose into child stories with granular ACs (f607dd45, standing justification).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Plan-review final triage (run 3: PASS, 0 blocking, 20 advisory — attestation over this material stands; this comment is the disposition record, deliberately non-material to preserve it). Pattern across runs 1-3: each round surfaced one level deeper; run-3 findings are child-story/ADR/test depth. DISPOSITION — FOLD INTO CHILD STORIES AT DECOMPOSITION (assigned): S2 story — assemble_merge_change_context signature must take the merge-path file list as an explicit input alongside auto_merge_diff + mergelist (fe192771, the round's top finding); cap-placement precision — MERGELIST_MAX_COMMITS is a count pre-cap, DIFF_CHAR_CAP governs the combined string, diff section truncated last (f95ed0c3); extend tests/unit _FakeGerrit with the four new client methods (f0f74d65); sequential REST fan-out latency budget + note (fc2fb799); observability.sh reason-dimension mechanics + the aws_cloudwatch_metric_alarm terraform resource, modelled on monitoring_s4b.tf (fe83fa83/f707a514/f487d44b/ff3c6696). S1 story — feature-branch-drivers membership policy constraints: initial members = repo admins + named operating agents, changes via admin-approved setup-project.sh edit (f8e6233f); back-out wording correction: 'no label/gate changes REQUIRED' — the copyCondition addition is inert absent merge changes (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE cannot match a non-merge patchset) and may be left or reverted (f51eb171/f90d5449). S3 story — verify checkout-gerrit-change-action fetches a merge refspec correctly; already the S3/S5 run-log done-definition (f6a211e0). JUSTIFIED, no action: g2p 'determine whether' cannot be resolved before 1fa8 ships g2p — the epic's stated planning horizon; S3 converts expectation to verified statement (f67e5550, standing from runs 1-2); 1fa8-deliverables-do-not-exist is the epic's explicit, user-directed assumption (f4b0eba6); local-git-diff alternative to REST fan-out rejected — Gerrit's REST auto-merge is the CANONICAL conflict-resolution base; recomputing locally reintroduces content-comparison divergence risk, the exact R4 fragility class this design eliminates (f32ad7cc); no feature-flag/canary for the merge path — acceptable: absent the new path a merge change fail-closes to -1 TODAY (409 guard), so worst-case regression equals current behavior, and back-out is redeploy-prior-image (f5d73737); pushMerge on a public-reachable Gerrit — bounded by the named narrow group + both label gates remain the enforcement point, recorded in the ADR (f1cabe96); DET compound-criteria — house epic style, standing justification (f607dd45).

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: Decomposed into 6 child stories (parented, gate-passing, dep-linked): S1 bored-tag-sale (fcaf — gate/ACL config; inherits f8e6233f membership policy + f51eb171/f90d5449 back-out wording), S2 urge-brook-plume (29a6 — bot merge path; inherits fe192771 signature, f95ed0c3 caps, f0f74d65 _FakeGerrit, fc2fb799 latency, f487d44b alarm; verify: make lint/test), S3 will-tile-plum (2e8a — g2p/CI verification; inherits f6a211e0 merge-refspec check; DO NOT start before 1fa8 closes), S4 perky-art-yodel (e7ab — docs + sweep), S5 leafy-vogue-ingot (a5b5 — 7-scenario live E2E; epic close gate), S6 norm-dam-swab (72b8 — policy/back-out/cost). Deps: S2<-S1; S4<-S1,S2,S3; S5<-S1,S2,S3; S6<-S5. Cross-epic 1fa8 dependency intentionally held at epic level only (validate flags cross-epic child deps). Each story needs its own plan review before claim.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: PLAN RE-VERIFICATION vs shipped 1fa8 (main @ 49ad69bcd, 2026-07-02). Plan remains ACCURATE — every S1-S6 work item still needed; zero scope collisions with what 1fa8 shipped. Confirmations: LLM-Review copyCondition still NO_CODE_CHANGE-only (S1 amendment needed as planned); NO [submit] section exists (S1 pin needed); setup-project.sh want-dict unchanged at line 57 (S1 reference exact); Verified label shipped with copyCondition=NO_CODE_CHANGE — IDENTICAL to our re-run posture decision, so S1 only records rationale; gerrit-verify.yaml exists with GERRIT_REFSPEC input + checkout-gerrit-change-action + concurrency keyed on CHANGE_ID cancel-in-progress (S3 verification targets all real now). NEW FACTS folded in: (1) Verified submit-requirement is authored INACTIVE (applicableIf=is:false) — flip is 1fa8's remaining step; S5 scenario 6 (CI blocks submit) REQUIRES the flip, correctly modeled by our epic-level depends_on 1fa8 (still in_progress). (2) S3 file-impact paths corrected: the g2p artifact is gerrit_to_platform.ini.template + materialize-g2p-config.sh (SSM-rendered at boot), not a plain ini; the template carries NO branch-filter key (only comment-added recheck mapping + token) — supports the branch-agnostic no-op hypothesis, g2p-source verification still required. (3) 1fa8 shipped runbooks two-vote-gate-rollback.md + g2p-ci-credentials.md and monitoring_1fa8.tf — S6 back-out should EXTEND two-vote-gate-rollback.md (not duplicate), S2's alarm should model on monitoring_1fa8.tf (fresher analog than monitoring_s4b.tf). (4) ATTESTATION DRIFT (expected + correct): 1fa8's change rewrote 3 of this epic's dep-pinned files (project.config, CONTRIBUTING.md, gerrit-aws-setup.md — hashes verified changed); the plan-review attestation is HMAC-certified but will read not-valid at claim → re-run review-plan at claim time.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
HANDOFF STATE (end of session 2) — 5 of 7 stories DONE (closed+signed): S1 bored-tag-sale, S2 urge-brook-plume, S3 will-tile-plum, S4 perky-art-yodel, and story 8903 gall-plume-adder (continuous auto-deploy, LIVE on box i-00880b2c7f13527c5). Epic stays in_progress; REMAINING: S5 leafy-vogue-ingot (OPEN, now unblocked by S3 close) + S6 norm-dam-swab (OPEN, depends on S5).

KEY OUTCOMES THIS SESSION:
- 8903 continuous auto-deploy: box-adapted (mirror clone + rsync into copy-based /opt/rebar; review-bot-only v1 auto-apply, config detect+signal). LIVE: rebar-autodeploy.timer enabled, review-bot redeployed to S2 code (S5 PREREQ SATISFIED — bot no longer pre-S2), self-heal + blast-radius + autonomous deploy all verified live; deploy_errors CloudWatch alarm applied; ADR 0026. Landed 229/230/231.
- S3: all 6 ACs proven live; AC4 (change 234 PS3 MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE -> LLM-Review CARRIED / Verified re-ran) + AC5 (concurrency cancel-in-progress) captured. Evidence committed to docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md (change 239).
- S4: feature-branch flow docs (CONTRIBUTING §4, CLAUDE.md, review-bot-ops merge-change ops, mirror-lock two-vote fix); PR-era sweep clean. Change 237.
- fern-belief-gap CLOSED (change 236: exclude feature/** from mirror push CI).

REMAINING WORK FOR NEXT SESSION:
- S5 (leafy-vogue-ingot): 7-scenario live E2E + a COMMITTED scenario script (alongside infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh) + COMMITTED evidence. NOTE: the completion verifier requires run-log evidence as a committed repo artifact (not just ticket comments) — extend docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md. Already proven: scenario 4 = S3 AC4; scenario 3 partial = S3 AC3; scenario 7 partial = S1/S2. Bot is S2-ready (prereq met).
- S6 (norm-dam-swab): policy + back-out + cost, from S5 data.
- Follow-ups: spy-luge-wool (bug, -1/INDETERMINATE root-cause via /joe-debug), sly-sloth-bay (task, emit merge_change_409_guard event — discovered_from S2).

FLAG: another session's Gerrit change 241 proposes 'ADR 0026-0029' (reconciler convergence) which COLLIDES with the merged ADR 0026 (autodeploy) — that session must renumber before submit.

ENV: AWS root authed; box reachable via SSM; Gerrit admin = JoeOakhartNava. Box: auto-deploy live+healthy, review-bot=S2, gerrit container untouched (ef56267f).

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: S5 live E2E run (session 3) — committed driver: infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh + feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh (HTTPS/REST, reuses reviewbot-e2e.sh conventions; 7 scenario fns). Throwaway branch feature/e2e-20260704. SCENARIO 2 (stacking) PASS live: change 248 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/248) pushed to refs/for/feature/e2e-20260704; LLM-Review=+1 (rebar review bot 1000002); reviewed diff = docs/e2e/s2-stacked-*.txt ONLY (delta_only, R1 proven — reviewed diff covers just its own delta, not accumulated history). CI (gerrit-verify run 28688669163) dispatched on the feature branch via g2p workflow_dispatch — confirms webhook+g2p branch-agnostic dispatch on feature/*.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCENARIO 1 (parallel siblings) PASS live — R1/R4 core. Two independent story changes off feature/e2e-20260704: change 251 (sibling A, https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/251) + change 252 (sibling B, https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/252). BOTH earned LLM-Review+1 (rebar review bot) AND Verified+1 (rebar CI bot; CI dispatched on the feature branch by g2p — webhook + branch-agnostic dispatch live-proven; wall-clock ~830-849s). Submitted A then B: feature-branch history shows A landed (4159e8be3), then B landed via a Gerrit-created MERGE COMMIT c0acd6091 'Merge sibling story B into feature/e2e-20260704' — MERGE_IF_NECESSARY submit strategy, ZERO manual rebase, ZERO vote wipe (R4: no invalidation fragility). CI wall-clock ~14min/run.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCENARIO 3 (merge-back) PASS live — R2/R3 core + RED/BLUE/GREEN replay(1-3) complete. Merge change 254 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/254): --no-ff merge of feature/e2e-20260704 (both S1 siblings accumulated) into refs/for/main. Bot reviewed ONLY /MERGE_LIST (merge-commit list) — NOT the story file contents = ZERO re-review of the already-attested stories (R1/R2). LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1 (CI green on the MERGE TREE, run dispatched via g2p). Submitted -> MERGED atomically as 2-parent merge commit 08194e1a9 (parents: main tip 4bd06245d + feature tip c0acd6091 containing A 4159e8be3 + B ffd3f6d1c). GitHub mirror main advanced to identical SHA 08194e1a9 via replication — ALL feature commits present. RED/BLUE/GREEN replay(scenarios 1-3): zero vote wipes, zero manual rebases, zero re-reviews of attested stories, single atomic landing. PROVEN.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
SCENARIO 7 (races + negatives) PASS live — ACL enforcement (ADR-0025 decision 3). Probed as NON-MEMBER identity rebar-review-bot (id 1000002; in Registered+Service Users, NOT feature-branch-drivers, NOT Administrators): (7c) feature/* branch CREATE -> HTTP 403 'not permitted: create on refs/heads/feature/neg-*', branch NOT created. (7b) --no-ff MERGE push to refs/for/main -> REJECTED 'commit d41c79b: you are not allowed to upload merges' (the exclusive pushMerge ACL; pushMerge=feature-branch-drivers only). POSITIVE contrast: JoeOakhartNava (feature-branch-driver) successfully merge-pushed+submitted change 254 (scenario 3). (7a) concurrent sibling submits behaving = scenario 1 (251+252 both landed, 2nd via merge-if-necessary). (7d) webhook backfill + concurrency cancel-in-progress = S3 AC5 precedent (change 234 PS1 run cancelled on PS2 push) + reconciler runbook.

SCENARIO 4 (vote-carry on re-merge) — proven live by S3 AC4 (docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md): change 234 PS3 MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE -> Gerrit 'Copied Votes: LLM-Review+1' CARRIED while Verified REMOVED + CI re-dispatched (run 28682539519); REWORK counter-case change 215 PS2 -> BOTH votes wiped. Identical mechanism/copyCondition this epic ships; cited rather than re-run to avoid redundant main churn.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
S5 STATUS + BLOCKER (session 3). PROVEN LIVE so far: scenario 1 (parallel siblings 251/252, merge-if-necessary no-rebase), scenario 2 (delta-only review, change 248), scenario 3 (merge-back 254 -> MERGED 08194e1a9, GitHub main advanced, /MERGE_LIST-only review = zero re-review), scenario 4 (cited S3 AC4 change 234 vote-carry), scenario 7 (ACL negatives: non-member branch-create 403 + merge-push 'not allowed to upload merges'; positive contrast change 254). Committed driver: infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh + feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh. Evidence in docs/88ab-feature-branch-evidence.md.

BLOCKER (filed as bug pelt-mead-aeon, discovered_from this epic): the rebar REVIEW BOT is broken system-wide since the ~01:38Z auto-deploy of main@151c2b69 (rebar.llm refactor 1fb66466d). It casts NO LLM-Review on any change (256+ all stuck, all agents), failing internally with 'cannot list /tmp/reviewbot-<x>/.tickets-tracker'. Container serves /health 200 so ADR-0026 auto-rollback didn't fire. This blocks scenario 6 (caller change 257 got Verified+1 but no LLM-Review) and blocks landing S5/S6 deliverables (both need LLM-Review). Left artifact: throwaway src/rebar/_e2e_s6_base.py landed on main (change 255) — scenario 6 aborted before its delete step; will clean up. Root-cause investigation + remediation decision pending.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCENARIO 6 (semantic conflict) PASS live — THE critical AC: 'Verified=-1 blocking submit on an empty auto-merge diff'. Construction: base module _e2e_s6_base_<stamp> landed on main (change 271); a PYTEST TEST importing it landed on dedicated branch feature/e2e-s6-<stamp> (change 273, bot-reviewed); main DELETED the base module (change 275, net-zero on main); then the --no-ff merge of the dedicated branch into main = change 276 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/276). RESULT: auto-merge delta = /MERGE_LIST only (clean merge, EMPTY author delta) -> LLM-Review=+1 PASSES (nothing to review); CI RED on the merged tree (pytest collection ImportError: the test imports the now-deleted module) -> Verified=-1; Verified SR UNSATISFIED -> submit BLOCKED. Two-vote complementarity proven: the review bot reviews the (empty) author delta while CI tests the full merged tree and catches the semantic break. Note: needed a pytest-collected test (not a plain module) because mypy has ignore_missing_imports=true, so only pytest's real import surfaces the break. Merge abandoned + dedicated branch torn down in cleanup.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: SCENARIO 5 (textual conflict) PASS live — ALL 7 SCENARIOS NOW PROVEN. fb-edit (change 277, MERGED to FB) + conflicting main-edit (change 278, MERGED to main) on the same file docs/e2e/s5-conflict.txt; the --no-ff merge of FB into main hit a real add/add textual conflict, resolved in the merge commit (change 279). The auto-merge delta is NON-EMPTY — reviewed files = [/MERGE_LIST, docs/e2e/s5-conflict.txt] — so the bot reviews the conflict-RESOLUTION content (LLM=1 VER=1). Deliberate contrast with scenario 6 (clean merge -> /MERGE_LIST-only -> empty author delta). S5 SCENARIO SUMMARY (all live): 1 parallel-siblings(251/252 merge-if-necessary no-rebase) | 2 stacking(248 delta-only) | 3 merge-back(254 atomic, GitHub main advanced, zero re-review) | 4 vote-carry(S3 AC4 change 234 MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE carries LLM, Verified re-runs) | 5 textual-conflict(279 non-empty resolution reviewed) | 6 semantic-conflict(276 empty delta, LLM passes, CI red, Verified=-1 BLOCKS submit — critical AC) | 7 ACL-negatives(403 branch-create + 'not allowed to upload merges' for non-member; concurrency+webhook precedent). Committed harness: infra/gerrit/feature-branch-e2e.sh + feature-branch-e2e-scenarios.sh.

compiled_state.description:
# Feature-branch accumulation: multi-story features assemble off-main under per-story LLM+CI gates and land via a reviewed merge change

## Context
Validation (session log apt-otter-urn, 2026-07-01) proved the current chain/topic flow cannot satisfy the five core requirements for multi-agent feature work: (R1) never review a huge diff in one review; (R2) accumulate a whole feature off-main and land it at once; (R3) every change reaching main passed LLM review; (R4) no invalidation fragility from content comparison or merge/rebase instability; (R5) the pattern must be validated by popular, actively-maintained OSS projects. Chains fail R4 (any rebase wipes votes under copyCondition=NO_CODE_CHANGE; the alternative TRIVIAL_REBASE carry IS content comparison). The adopted pattern is the OpenDev-documented **server-side feature branch + reviewed merge change**: stories are small changes reviewed INTO refs/heads/feature/<name> as they pass (attestations are consumed immediately into branch history — no vote ever needs to survive a rebase; branch history is the durable accumulator), then one --no-ff merge commit of the branch into main is pushed to refs/for/main as its own reviewed change, gated identically, and submitted atomically. Feature branches live only in Gerrit (replication pushes main+tags), so nothing reaches GitHub until the merge change lands.

**ASSUMES epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) is completed and merged**: the `Verified` label exists, cast by GitHub Actions via gerrit-to-platform hooks (patchset-created / comment-added `recheck`), the `gerrit-verify.yaml` workflow exists, and the two-vote submit requirement `label:LLM-Review=MAX AND label:Verified=MAX AND -has:unresolved` is active. (References to g2p config and `gerrit-verify.yaml` below are to those 1fa8 deliverables; they intentionally do not exist yet.)

### Decision log (2026-07-01 validation + web research; citations in apt-otter-urn)
- **OSS validation (R5):** OpenDev Infra Manual documents this flow verbatim (server-side branch, pushMerge ACL, `git merge --no-ff` pushed to refs/for/master); live use: openstack/swift feature/mpu + feature/threaded through 2026 with story-sized reviewed changes + reviewed catch-up merges; Qt runs reviewed merge changes live (qt-creator, June 2026). Caveat adopted as policy: keep feature branches SHORT-LIVED (OpenDev: "specifically not for sustained long term development"; Qt retreated from routine long-lived-branch merges in 2020).
- **Merge-change review base = Gerrit auto-merge** (conflict resolutions + manual edits only — usually near-empty), NOT the whole feature diff (R1). REST: `files/` with no `parent` param and per-file `diff` default to the auto-merge base for merge commits (rest-api-changes.html#list-files: "If not specified, the response contains a map of the files different in the auto merge result"); `mergelist` lists integrated commits. **This default is the riskiest assumption in the plan — S2 verifies it against the running Gerrit 3.14.1 with a captured fixture BEFORE building on it.** **TRAP:** bare `GET .../revisions/{rev}/patch` on a merge commit returns HTTP 409 ("Revision has more than 1 parent", GetPatch.java) — today our voter would fail-closed -1 on any merge change (safe, but blocks the pattern); `patch?parent=1` silently yields the ENTIRE feature diff (violates R1). The bot needs a distinct merge-change path.
- **UI default diff base for merge commits is FIRST_PARENT** (whole feature) — fine for humans; never design assuming reviewers land on the conflicts-only view.
- **LLM-Review copyCondition gains `changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`** — Gerrit-native, purpose-built ("useful if you don't want to trigger CI or human verification again if your target branch moved forward", config-labels.html). A re-merge because main advanced keeps the +1; a changed feature tip is a REWORK and correctly re-reviews. Not a content-comparison heuristic (R4). S1 proves the config push is accepted by the running 3.14.1; the carry behavior itself is proven live in S5 scenario 4.
- **Verified deliberately does NOT carry on re-merge** (stays NO_CODE_CHANGE only): CI re-runs on the new combined tree via patchset-created. This is the semantic-conflict mitigation — a textually-clean merge shows an EMPTY auto-merge diff even when semantically broken (Chromium's standing objection to merges), so the LLM cannot catch it; CI must. Deliberate label divergence mirroring roles: LLM reviews content (unchanged on a clean re-merge), CI validates the tree (changed).
- **Submit type pinned explicitly**: `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` (currently unset/inherited — an unpinned gap flagged in validation; FF-only or cherry-pick/rebase-always on main would defeat the atomic-merge design). S1 records the CURRENT effective inherited value before pinning and applies the pin in a quiet window (no open changes targeting main), with its own back-out note (S6).
- `receive.rejectImplicitMerges` is unaffected — it explicitly exempts real merge commits (config-project-config.html).

## Locked design
- **Branch namespace** `refs/heads/feature/<name>`, created/deleted server-side (UI/REST); `Create Reference` + `Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` AND `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` + `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*` all granted to ONE named narrow group (`feature-branch-drivers`), membership policy recorded in the ADR (label gates remain the enforcement point; the ACL bounds attack surface + review-cost amplification). Branch deletion after merge-back is a scripted/manual step (Gerrit has no auto-cleanup).
- **Story loop:** small changes target `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/<name>`; the review-bot webhook is branch-agnostic per config (webhooks.config has no branch filter; proven live by S5 scenario 1) and the submit requirements have no branch clause; g2p dispatch for `feature/*` refs is EXPECTED branch-agnostic and is VERIFIED in S3 (its done-definition) before the design relies on it — so BOTH votes gate every feature-branch change (R3); submit into the branch as each passes. Parallel agents in separate worktrees both target the branch; MERGE_IF_NECESSARY merges siblings without any manual rebase (R4).
- **Catch-up merges** main→feature as reviewed merge changes on the branch (OpenDev recipe), on a documented cadence; branch-lifetime cap.
- **Merge-back:** in the merge worktree, FIRST install the Gerrit commit-msg hook (`curl -sLo .git/hooks/commit-msg https://<gerrit-host>/tools/hooks/commit-msg && chmod +x .git/hooks/commit-msg` — a fresh worktree/clone does not have it, and without it the amend adds no Change-Id and the push is rejected); then `--no-ff` merge commit, Change-Id stamped via `GIT_EDITOR=/bin/true git commit --amend`, pushed to refs/for/main under pushMerge; bot reviews vs auto-merge + mergelist; CI builds/tests the merge tree; both labels gate; submit lands the feature atomically; replication advances GitHub main; branch deleted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] **S1 — Gate + ACL config** (`infra/gerrit/project.config`, `infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh`, declarative): LLM-Review `copyCondition = changekind:NO_CODE_CHANGE OR changekind:MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` (push accepted by the running 3.14.1); Verified copyCondition UNCHANGED (re-run posture) with the rationale recorded; record the current effective inherited submit type (Gerrit REST/ssh query, noted on the ticket) THEN pin `[submit] action = merge if necessary` + `mergeContent = true` in a quiet window; ACLs: `Create Reference`/`Delete Reference` on `refs/heads/feature/*` + `Push Merge Commit` on `refs/for/refs/heads/main` and `refs/for/refs/heads/feature/*`, ALL bound to the named `feature-branch-drivers` group — including extending `setup-project.sh`'s managed-groups `want` dict (~line 57, currently only Administrators + Service Users) so the group is created/resolved declaratively; ADR amending ADR-0013 (+ 1fa8's Verified ADR) recording the merge-change gate, label-divergence rationale, and the group membership policy; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md` gate/ACL sections updated. Verified live on the running Gerrit.
- [ ] **S2 — Review-bot merge-change path.** FIRST (riskiest assumption): capture a live fixture from the running Gerrit 3.14.1 proving `files/` with no `parent` returns the auto-merge-base file map for a merge commit (record the fixture in tests + cite rest-api-changes.html#list-files); only then build on it. Explicit edit targets: `voter.py` — merge detection via `revisions/{rev}/commit` parent count (>=2) runs INSIDE `review_and_vote` immediately after the existing-vote check and BEFORE any diff fetch, so the merge path is taken identically for webhook, reconciler-backfill, and `/rerun` triggers (reconcile.py itself is expected to need NO change — asserted by a test routing a merge-change event through the backfill path; the one extra `commit` GET per review is an accepted overhead — negligible next to the LLM call, and an error on it fails closed); `gerrit_client.py` — new `get_commit`, `get_merge_files` (no parent), `get_file_diff` (auto-merge base), `get_mergelist` methods; NEVER bare `/patch` on merges (a unit test asserts the 409 path stays fail-closed if ever hit); ANY merge-path REST failure (commit / files / diff / mergelist) → fail-closed -1 carrying the existing coverage-gap (infra) message tag, mirroring the current posture. Merge-context contract: a new `assemble_merge_change_context(auto_merge_diff, mergelist)` co-located with `DiffContext` in `src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py` (reusing `DIFF_CHAR_CAP`; mergelist section capped by a named constant `MERGELIST_MAX_COMMITS = 100` with truncation notice), producing a documented format: `## Merge context` (integrated-commit subject lines) then `## Auto-merge diff`; the COMBINED string is capped at DIFF_CHAR_CAP with the mergelist section capped first, and per-file diffs are fetched only until the combined cap is reached (remaining files listed undiffed with a truncation notice — bounds the per-file REST fan-out); the empty-auto-merge-diff case (clean merge) is explicitly defined — review proceeds on mergelist-only context and the robot comment states the conflict delta was empty. **Data flow into the gate is unchanged:** the assembled string is passed as `diff_text` through the existing seam — `adapter.code_review_decision(diff_text=…)` → `produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=…)` — no runner/gate-machinery changes. Robot comment: the merge-change variant REUSES the existing `_TAG_SUFFIXES` verdict keys in `adapter.py`, appending `(merge-change, N integrated commits)` to the existing suffix text (no new verdict vocabulary), e.g. `[LLM-Review: PASS (merge-change, 12 integrated commits)]`. Observability: structured journald log events `merge_change_review` (mode activation + integrated-commit count), `merge_change_409_guard` (ERROR), `mergelist_fetch_error` (ERROR), published as a CloudWatch metric `review_bot_merge_change_errors` (reason-tagged) via `infra/scripts/observability.sh` exactly as `voter_errors` is, plus a "signals to watch" entry in the runbook. Unit + fixture tests for all of the above.
- [ ] **S3 — CI coverage on feature branches + merge changes** (assumes 1fa8 live): determine from g2p source/docs whether `gerrit_to_platform.ini` filters dispatch by branch (replace the design's expectation with a verified statement: either the confirmed required `feature/*` config, or a confirmed no-config-needed no-op, recorded in the ini spec); done-definition: a `feature/*` patchset-created event triggers `gerrit-verify.yaml` AND a merge change into main gets CI on the actual merge tree (checkout-gerrit-change-action fetching the merge refspec), each proven with run-log evidence; Verified re-runs (not carries) on a re-merge; `concurrency: cancel-in-progress` behaves sanely across branch + merge changes.
- [ ] **S4 — Documentation sweep**: `CONTRIBUTING.md` gains a "Multi-story features (feature branches)" section (branch creation request path, story loop targeting the branch, catch-up merge recipe, merge-back recipe with the commit-msg hook install as an explicit prerequisite step + the Change-Id amend + merge-conflict resolution guidance + how to abandon a bad merge change and start over, pushMerge-refusal troubleshooting, a "missing Change-Id on merge push" troubleshooting entry, branch deletion); `CLAUDE.md` Git-workflow section (~line 410) gains the feature-branch flow + when-agents-should-use-it guidance (multi-agent parallel features vs single small changes), including the hook-install-in-fresh-worktree note; `README.md` + install docs (`docs/local-dev-env.md`, CONTRIBUTING one-time setup) verified current for the two-vote + feature-branch world; `docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md`; `infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md` (merge-change ops: 409 semantics, /rerun on merges, the new log signals, bot-code rollback = redeploy the prior review-bot image); repo-wide sweep for stale text implying the single-change amend loop is the ONLY flow or describing PR-era GitHub practices (grep "pull request", "gh pr", "PR merge", single-change-only phrasing) — the sweep's hit list is recorded on the ticket and every hit is fixed or has a recorded justification.
- [ ] **S5 — Live multi-agent E2E validation** on the running Gerrit (scripted alongside `infra/gerrit/reviewbot-e2e.sh`; every scenario's change URLs + votes recorded on this ticket): (1) **parallel siblings** — two agents in separate worktrees push independent story changes to `feature/e2e-<date>`; both earn LLM-Review + Verified and submit WITHOUT either rebasing (second submit succeeds via MERGE_IF_NECESSARY with no manual rebase; this also live-proves webhook + g2p branch-agnostic dispatch); (2) **stacking** — a third agent pulls the branch (both stories in history) and pushes a change on top; the reviewed diff covers ONLY its delta; (3) **merge-back** — `--no-ff` merge change to main: bot reviews the auto-merge delta only (assert NOT the whole-feature diff), CI green on the merge tree, both votes present, submit lands atomically, replication advances GitHub main with all commits, branch deleted; (4) **vote-carry on re-merge** — land an unrelated change on main mid-flight, push a re-merge (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE): LLM-Review +1 CARRIES, Verified RE-RUNS; then change the feature tip: BOTH wiped (REWORK); (5) **textual conflict** — overlapping feature-vs-main edits resolved in the merge commit; bot reviews the non-empty auto-merge delta; (6) **semantic conflict** — textually-clean but semantically broken combination (e.g. rename on main + new call-site on feature): auto-merge diff empty, LLM-Review passes, CI red → Verified=-1 BLOCKS submit (proves two-vote complementarity); (7) **races + negatives** — concurrent submits into the branch behave (MERGE_IF_NECESSARY or clear conflict signal); a merge push WITHOUT pushMerge is refused; branch creation outside `feature-branch-drivers` is refused; the reconciler backfills a dropped webhook for a feature-branch change (including a merge change, exercising the transparent merge detection).
- [ ] **S6 — Process + back-out**: branch-lifetime cap + catch-up cadence documented; stale-branch policy — a scripted inventory check flags `feature/*` branches inactive beyond the cap (merged-back OR abandoned) for owner-confirmed deletion, recorded in the runbook; back-out documented + tested (revoke pushMerge + feature/* ACLs restores the single-change-only flow with NO label/gate changes; the submit-type pin back-out = remove the `[submit]` block and confirm the inherited value matches the S1-recorded prior state; bot code back-out = redeploy the prior review-bot image); cost/latency recorded as a "Cost & latency" section in the amending ADR with, from the S5 runs: median CI wall-clock per merge change, count of LLM re-reviews avoided via MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE carry vs CI re-runs incurred across the scenarios, and per-review LLM cost if surfaced by the runner.

## Success Criteria
- R1–R5 all hold end-to-end and are each demonstrated by at least one S5 scenario (R1: scenarios 2+3; R2: 3; R3: 1+3+6; R4: 1+4; R5: recorded OSS evidence in apt-otter-urn + ADR).
- The RED/BLUE/GREEN replay (scenarios 1–3) completes with zero vote wipes, zero manual rebases, zero re-reviews of already-attested stories, and a single atomic landing on main.
- The S4 sweep hit-list on the ticket shows zero unjustified stale-practice references remaining.

## Sequencing & risk
S1 (additive config) → S2 (bot merge path MUST precede any real merge change — today a merge change 409s to a fail-closed -1; S2 opens with the files/-default fixture verification, the plan's riskiest assumption) → S3 (needs 1fa8 live) → S4 (after semantics locked) → S5 (last; gates epic close) ; S6 folds into S4/S5 outputs. Risk: auto-merge review blindness to semantic conflicts is accepted and mitigated BY DESIGN via the Verified re-run posture (S3) + scenario 6 proof. Risk: long-lived branches rot — mitigated by lifetime cap + catch-up cadence + stale-branch inventory (S6). Risk: g2p branch filtering silently skipping feature/* (would break R3) — closed by S3's verified statement + run-log done-definition.

## Out of scope
- Cherry-pick-down release-branch model (Qt QUIP-16) — solves release maintenance, not feature accumulation.
- Automated feature-branch cleanup service (scripted/manual deletion only; the S6 inventory check is a script, not a service).
- Changes to relation-chain/topic behavior for SMALL work (a 2–3 change chain remains fine; feature branches are for multi-story features).
- Any GitHub-side workflow (the mirror stays read-only; PRs stay dead).
- Stacked-diff client tooling.

## Key references
- Validation + research record: session log apt-otter-urn (c905-a38c-0419-448e) — OSS citations, REST mechanics, corrections.
- OpenDev Infra Manual "Project Driver's Guide" (feature branches + merge-back recipe + pushMerge ACL); openstack/swift feature/mpu live changes; Qt Merge Guidelines + QUIP-16.
- Gerrit 3.x docs: config-labels.html (MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE), rest-api-changes.html (files/diff parent semantics incl. #list-files auto-merge default, mergelist, GetPatch 409), access-control.html (pushMerge, Create/Delete Reference), user-review-ui.html (auto-merge base, Merge List), config-project-config.html (rejectImplicitMerges exempts real merges).
- Local: infra/gerrit/{project.config,setup-project.sh,reviewbot-e2e.sh}, src/rebar/review_bot/{voter.py,gerrit_client.py,adapter.py,reconcile.py}, src/rebar/llm/code_review/assemble.py, infra/scripts/observability.sh, ADR-0010/0013, epic 1fa8 (smoky-flare-slug) + its ADRs, CONTRIBUTING.md, CLAUDE.md §Git workflow, docs/gerrit-aws-setup.md, infra/runbooks/review-bot-ops.md.
